name: ETH State Change Momentum Hold v1
thesis: ETH 1h is the best-fit primary lane for testing state-change momentum after regime improvement and post-break hold. The edge should come from accepted structure change followed by hold, not from raw breakout touching alone.
invalidation_condition: Abort if hold logic cannot generate enough participation, if momentum and hold filters conflict, or if continuation collapses after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A local state shift occurs after balance, price breaks through structure, and the move holds instead of immediately mean reverting back into range.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in mostly choppy intraday conditions, a real state change can create several bars of persistence if the first reclaim of old balance fails.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is the best-fit lane because state changes and post-break holds are inherently faster structures and should produce denser participation than 4h.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains purely rotational, post-break holds will fade quickly and the continuation thesis will fail.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but persistence is short, shorten holding logic before tightening the trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; momentum improvement and structural hold are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad hold logic instead of exact breakout-candle conditions

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
